note: Two tiers. Trigger queries live in trigger-eval.json (run via skill-creator's Description Optimization loop). The behavioral cases below are judgment-shaped: fixtures/ hold pre-states, assertions/ hold graded invariants. Run each behavioral case N=3-5 times into scratch workspaces and report per-invariant pass rate + content variance (see ../references/behavioral-eval-scaffold.md).

evals.prompt: /akira wander on the diff in fixtures/wander-question-quality/input/ (repo context: a python service that caches user lookups)
evals.judgment_path: does wander find the DECISIONS the diff walked past (unbounded cache, no invalidation, error path swallowed) rather than restating what the code does?
evals.expected_output: 3-5 pointed questions about intent/edge-cases/missing-decisions, no findings list, no file edits

evals.prompt: /akira dao on the diff in fixtures/dao-triage-tiering/input/ (a test-backed python repo). Triage the findings into blast-radius tiers.
evals.judgment_path: does dao tier a dead-import removal as low (auto-apply candidate) and a changed error-path/control-flow finding as high (surface-only)?
evals.expected_output: the nit is auto-applied behind the test gate; the logic finding is surfaced only, never applied; run summary written; nothing committed

evals.prompt: /akira dao on the diff in fixtures/dao-revert-on-failure/input/ (a test-backed python repo). One low-radius fix is deliberately wrong and will break the test suite.
evals.judgment_path: when an auto-applied low-radius fix fails the test suite, dao reverts THAT hunk only and records it as attempted-reverted, leaving no failing tree.
evals.expected_output: the bad fix is reverted, tree left clean of it, run summary lists it under 'Attempted, reverted on test failure', nothing committed
